title: Wedding Venues in Maine
headline: A Maine Wedding Venue Worth the Drive
subhead: 66 acres in Fayette, Maine. Two restored barns, a farmhouse that sleeps 10, and a full weekend that's entirely yours.
description: Looking for wedding venues in Maine? South Road Farm is a private 66-acre property in Fayette, Maine offering full-weekend weddings for up to 150 guests. Two restored barns, on-site accommodations, and no required vendors.
heroImageAlt: Wedding ceremony at South Road Farm under open sky
intro: Maine has no shortage of beautiful places to get married. What's harder to find is a place that gives you the whole weekend — not just a Saturday. South Road Farm in Fayette is 66 acres of open sky, two restored barns, and a farmhouse that sleeps your people. It's available May through October, one wedding at a time.
proximity: Located in Fayette, Maine — roughly 30 minutes from Augusta and about an hour north of Portland.

bodyContent.heading: One wedding per weekend. Always.
bodyContent.text: South Road Farm books one couple per weekend, by design. The 66 acres are yours from Friday afternoon to Sunday morning — the ceremony lawn, both barns, the courtyard, the farmhouse. No shared parking lots, no strangers wandering through your cocktail hour, no one flipping the barn for the next event at midnight. The weekend has room to breathe because we built it that way.

bodyContent.heading: A property with a real history
bodyContent.text: Joseph Watson settled this land on July 4, 1793. The post-and-beam framing still standing in the main barn went up in the early 1800s. The farm ran continuously as a working farm until 2018 — longer than Maine has been a state. What you see now is the result of several years of careful restoration, done largely by hand by Dave, a lifelong carpenter and co-owner of the property. The history isn't decoration. It's in the materials.

quote.text: The moonrise over the barn and stable transported me to a time when its pale light stretched a farmer's day. Morning coffee on the deck, overlooking the hills to Androscoggin Lake in Wayne, carried me further back, to when my ancestors first settled there in the late 18th century.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: South Road Farm sits in the central Maine lakes region — one of the quietest and most beautiful parts of the state. Echo Lake and Parker Pond are within a few miles, offering swimming, kayaking, and the kind of morning loon calls that become part of your wedding weekend memories. The Kennebec Highlands trail network is close by for guests who want to hike. Augusta, Maine's capital city, is about 30 minutes away, with restaurants along Water Street including Otto's on the River and The Oak Table & Bar for rehearsal dinners or welcome gatherings. For guests coming from away, the drive through Readfield and into Fayette on Routes 17 and 41 is genuinely beautiful — rolling hills, farmland, and lake views all the way in.

title: Barn Wedding Venues in Maine
headline: A Barn Built in the 1800s. Restored by Hand.
subhead: Two historic barns on 66 private acres in central Maine. One wedding per weekend. Yours from Friday to Sunday.
description: Searching for barn wedding venues in Maine? South Road Farm features two hand-restored 19th-century barns on 66 private acres in Fayette, Maine. Full-weekend rentals, farmhouse accommodations for up to 10, and capacity for 150 guests.
heroImageAlt: Cocktail hour outside the barn at South Road Farm
intro: There's a particular quality to old timber — the way it holds light, the way it carries sound. The barns at South Road Farm were built in the early 1800s. The post-and-beam framing is original. They've been restored by hand over the past several years by Dave, a lifelong carpenter and co-owner of the property. What you see is what happens when someone builds something for themselves, not for a contractor's schedule.
proximity: Located in Fayette, Maine — in the heart of central Maine's lakes region.

bodyContent.heading: Two barns, two purposes
bodyContent.text: The main barn is the reception space — open, high-ceilinged, capable of holding up to 150 guests for dinner and dancing. The secondary barn is smaller, well-suited for cocktail hour, a rehearsal dinner, or a Sunday morning farewell brunch. Having both means the weekend can move through different spaces without feeling cramped or rushed.

bodyContent.heading: Restored, not renovated
bodyContent.text: A lot of barn venues are barns in name only — metal buildings with barn aesthetic bolted on. The structures at South Road Farm are original 19th-century New England post-and-beam construction, sitting on a granite foundation hauled by oxen from quarries in Jay, Maine. The restoration preserved what was there. A windstorm took the entire roof off one of the barns during the process — not just the shingles, the full timber framing — and the team rebuilt it from scratch rather than replace it with something modern. You can't fake that kind of commitment.

quote.text: South Road Farm is where I found my way back to my roots. Years after leaving Wayne, I returned to my aunt and uncle's farm in Fayette for my wedding — a four-day celebration grounded in family, friends, and tradition.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: Fayette is a small town defined by its lakes — Echo Lake, Parker Pond, Flying Pond, and a dozen smaller ponds that catch the light differently in every season. The Kennebec Highlands trail network passes nearby, with hikes at Blueberry Hill and Mount Pisgah offering views across the lake country. Surry Hill Community Forest, just minutes away, has trails to an open summit with views stretching to the White Mountains on clear days. For guests staying in the area, Augusta's Water Street has become a genuine dining destination — Cushnoc Brewing Company, State Lunch, and Otto's on the River all do well for rehearsal dinners and welcome gatherings.

title: Outdoor Wedding Venues in Maine
headline: Ceremony Under Open Sky. Dancing Until Midnight.
subhead: Two ceremony sites, 66 acres, and Maine light that does most of the work for you.
description: Looking for outdoor wedding venues in Maine? South Road Farm offers two distinct outdoor ceremony sites on 66 acres in Fayette, Maine — an open field with panoramic views and a wooded grove for something more intimate.
heroImageAlt: Outdoor wedding ceremony on the lawn at South Road Farm
intro: South Road Farm has two ceremony sites. One is an open field with views in every direction — the kind of setting where you forget there's a photographer because you're too busy looking around. The other is tucked into the treeline, quieter, more sheltered. Both have a hand-built wooden arch and land that's been held in one form or another since 1793.
proximity: Located in Fayette, Maine — an easy drive from Augusta, Lewiston, and the greater Portland area.

bodyContent.heading: Two sites, two entirely different feelings
bodyContent.text: The open field ceremony site sits on elevated ground with views in every direction — sky, hills, and the kind of Maine light that photographers come specifically to find. It's best for couples who want grandeur and openness. The wooded ceremony site is accessed by a path through the trees and feels completely private, like the rest of the world has stepped back. Both sites use the same hand-built wooden arch. The choice comes down to what kind of feeling you want for the moment.

bodyContent.heading: The full outdoor picture
bodyContent.text: The ceremony is only the beginning of the outdoor experience at South Road Farm. Cocktail hour happens on the lawn and in the courtyard — fire pits lit, the bar open, live music filling the field at golden hour. Dinner can move between the barns and tented outdoor space. Late night finds people drifting back outside around the fires. The 66 acres aren't a backdrop. They're active participants in the whole weekend.

quote.text: When I returned home, I longed for the night sounds of the farm — the steady chorus of crickets, the throaty calls of tree frogs, the sudden screech of an owl. The moonrise over the barn and stable transported me to a time when its pale light stretched a farmer's day.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: The landscape around Fayette is part of what makes the outdoor experience at South Road Farm work as well as it does. Echo Lake's public beach is minutes away; Parker Pond offers quiet paddling at sunrise. The Kennebec Highlands rise to the west, with trail networks connecting to Blueberry Hill and Mount Pisgah — short hikes with views that give wedding guests a sense of the broader lake country they're in. The light in central Maine in September and October — when the maples and birches turn — is something photographers specifically travel to find. Planning a late September or October wedding date means the surrounding landscape is doing as much visual work as the property itself.

title: Wedding Venues Near Portland, Maine
headline: Far Enough to Feel Like Somewhere. Close Enough to Get There.
subhead: South Road Farm is about an hour north of Portland — and worth every mile.
description: Looking for wedding venues near Portland, Maine? South Road Farm in Fayette, Maine is a full-weekend barn wedding venue about an hour north of Portland, with 66 acres, two restored barns, and on-site farmhouse accommodations.
heroImageAlt: Food trucks and guests at South Road Farm at dusk
intro: Most of the barn venues closest to Portland are booked years out and priced accordingly. South Road Farm is about an hour north — in Fayette, in the central Maine lakes region — and that distance is part of the point. Couples who come here are making a deliberate choice to go somewhere. Their guests are too. The weekend feels different when everyone has traveled to be there.
proximity: About an hour north of Portland, 30 minutes from Augusta, 45 minutes from Lewiston-Auburn.

bodyContent.heading: A destination that's actually reachable
bodyContent.text: Far enough to feel like you've left the city behind — and close enough that no one has to fly. Guests coming from Portland can make an easy drive up Route 202. Guests coming from Boston are looking at about three hours. There are hotels and Airbnbs within 20–30 minutes of the farm in Augusta and the lakes region, and the drive through central Maine in any season is genuinely beautiful.

bodyContent.heading: What Portland-area couples tend to find
bodyContent.text: The venues immediately around Portland have something South Road Farm doesn't: proximity to the city. What South Road Farm has that they don't: 66 uninterrupted acres, a full weekend with no shared use, a farmhouse included in the rental, and pricing that reflects being off the beaten path rather than being five minutes from the Old Port. For couples who want their wedding to feel like a genuine escape, that hour north is the feature, not the bug.

quote.text: After the wedding, I kept returning to the farm, drawn back again and again. I left my job, with its constant travel to Boston and New Hampshire, and started a small-batch pesto business with my aunt — South Road Farm Pesto, growing many of the herbs on the farm. Each time I headed home to southern Maine, I felt the pull to turn around. In time, I did.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: The drive from Portland to South Road Farm is itself part of the experience. Route 202 north through Winthrop and Wayne follows the edge of several lakes — Maranacook Lake, Androscoggin Lake, Echo Lake — before arriving in Fayette. Most guests find the drive passes quickly. Augusta, about 30 minutes from the farm, serves well as a guest hub — it has hotels from most major chains, a growing downtown dining scene along Water Street, and easy access back to I-95 for guests traveling from southern Maine, Boston, or beyond. The Oak Table & Bar and Otto's on the River on the Kennebec are both well-regarded options for welcome dinners.

title: Wedding Venues Near Augusta, Maine
headline: Central Maine's Full-Weekend Wedding Venue
subhead: 30 minutes from Augusta. 66 acres, two barns, and a farmhouse that's yours for the weekend.
description: Searching for wedding venues near Augusta, Maine? South Road Farm in Fayette is just 30 minutes from Augusta — a private 66-acre property with two restored barns, farmhouse accommodations, and full-weekend rentals for up to 150 guests.
heroImageAlt: Couple at the ceremony arch at South Road Farm
intro: If you're based in central Maine or planning a wedding accessible to guests coming through Augusta, South Road Farm is the closest full-weekend barn venue in the region. It's 30 minutes from the State House, on a quiet road in Fayette, on land that's been continuously worked since the late 18th century.
proximity: Located in Fayette, Maine — approximately 30 minutes from Augusta, 45 minutes from Waterville, and 45 minutes from Lewiston.

bodyContent.heading: The closest full-weekend venue to central Maine
bodyContent.text: Most full-weekend barn venues in Maine cluster around Portland or the midcoast. South Road Farm is the rare option in central Maine — close to Augusta, close to the lakes region, and genuinely easy to reach from Waterville, Lewiston, Skowhegan, and Farmington. If your family is central Maine-based, this is the venue where no one has to drive more than an hour.

bodyContent.heading: Augusta as your guest hub
bodyContent.text: Augusta has a solid range of hotels, which makes it an easy base for guests who aren't staying on-site. The drive from Augusta to the farm is about 30 minutes on quiet roads through Readfield and into Fayette — a genuinely pleasant drive in any season. Many couples set up a hotel block in Augusta and arrange shuttle service to and from the farm for the weekend.

quote.text: Through South Road Farm, the whispers of my ancestors brought me home.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: Augusta is a 30-minute drive from South Road Farm through some of central Maine's most scenic countryside — Routes 17 and 41 through Winthrop and Readfield, past Maranacook Lake and through the quiet farmland that defines this part of the state. Downtown Augusta has seen a genuine revival in recent years. Water Street along the Kennebec River has Cushnoc Brewing Company, Otto's on the River, State Lunch, and The Oak Table & Bar — all solid options for rehearsal dinners or welcome gatherings. For guests who want to explore the area, Echo Lake's public beach, the Kennebec Highlands trail network, and Parker Pond are all within 15–20 minutes of the farm.

title: Full Weekend Wedding Venues in Maine
headline: Most Weddings Give You a Saturday. This Is a Weekend.
subhead: Friday through Sunday. The property is yours. One wedding at a time.
description: Looking for full-weekend wedding venues in Maine? South Road Farm offers exclusive full-weekend rentals in Fayette, Maine — Friday through Sunday, one couple at a time, with 66 acres, two barns, and a farmhouse that sleeps 10.
heroImageAlt: Late night at South Road Farm with food truck glowing through the barn door
intro: The full-weekend format changes everything about how a wedding feels. There's no rush to finish setup by noon Saturday. No clearing out by midnight so the next couple can come in. The weekend at South Road Farm starts Friday evening — welcome drinks in the courtyard, the farmhouse filling up — and winds down Sunday morning over coffee. The celebration has room to breathe.
proximity: Located in Fayette, Maine. Available May through October.

bodyContent.heading: How the weekend unfolds
bodyContent.text: Friday: guests and wedding party arrive throughout the afternoon. The farmhouse fills up. Welcome drinks happen in the courtyard — informal, no agenda, just the first night together. Saturday: the main event. Ceremony, cocktail hour, dinner, dancing, late night. Sunday: no one's in a hurry. The farmhouse kitchen fills up slowly. Coffee. Last night's stories. The kind of morning that most weddings skip entirely because the venue needs you out. The three-day arc is the product.

bodyContent.heading: Why the farmhouse matters
bodyContent.text: The farmhouse sleeping 10 guests is included in every weekend rental — not an add-on, not an upgrade. Having the wedding party or immediate family on-site changes the texture of the weekend. You're not driving back to a hotel at midnight. You're staying in the place where it happened. Sunday morning breakfast in the farmhouse kitchen, still on the property, is something couples consistently mention as one of their favorite parts of the whole weekend.

quote.text: For months, I went to the farm each weekend to plan the wedding. Somewhere in that time, something in me began to stir. The feeling was both distant and familiar.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: Part of what makes the full-weekend format work at South Road Farm is what surrounds it. Fayette's chain of lakes — Echo Lake, Parker Pond, Flying Pond — gives guests natural things to do between events. Kayak rentals are easy to arrange. The Kennebec Highlands trail network has hikes suited to any level. Augusta is 30 minutes away for guests who want a proper dinner out the night before or after. The Fayette Country Store is a genuine local institution for morning coffee and provisions. The pace of central Maine in summer and fall — unhurried, deeply quiet, with long evenings and dark skies — suits the full-weekend format better than almost any other part of New England.

title: Barn Wedding Venues in New England
headline: A New England Barn Wedding Done Differently
subhead: Two 19th-century barns. 66 acres. A full weekend in central Maine.
description: Searching for barn wedding venues in New England? South Road Farm in Fayette, Maine offers two hand-restored historic barns on 66 private acres — full-weekend rentals, farmhouse accommodations, and capacity for up to 150 guests.
heroImageAlt: First kiss under the wooden ceremony arch at South Road Farm
intro: New England has more barn wedding venues than anywhere in the country. Most of them look the same: exposed beams, string lights, a cocktail hour on a patio. South Road Farm shares the bones of that tradition — post-and-beam construction from the early 1800s, still standing — but the experience is different. You're not renting a barn for a day. You're renting 66 acres and a weekend.
proximity: Located in Fayette, Maine. Accessible from Boston (3 hours), Portland ME (about 1 hour), Burlington VT (2.5 hours), and Manchester NH (2.5 hours).

bodyContent.heading: What makes a New England barn worth traveling for
bodyContent.text: The best New England barn venues have something the newer ones don't: age. The patina of old timber, the irregular geometry of hand-hewn beams, the sense that the space has held other things before it held a wedding. South Road Farm has been on this land since 1793 — the barns predate Maine statehood. That's not a marketing line. It's in the materials. The granite foundation was hauled by oxen. The framing was raised by hand. You can feel the difference.

bodyContent.heading: Maine versus the rest of New England
bodyContent.text: Vermont and New Hampshire have excellent barn venues. They also have very high demand and pricing to match. Maine tends to offer more property for the same spend — more acreage, more privacy, more genuine countryside. The tradeoff is distance from Boston and New York. For couples who want the quintessential New England barn experience and are willing to make the drive north, Maine consistently delivers something the more convenient options can't: the feeling of having actually gone somewhere.

quote.text: The moonrise over the barn and stable transported me to a time when its pale light stretched a farmer's day. Morning coffee on the deck, overlooking the hills to Androscoggin Lake in Wayne, carried me further back, to when my ancestors first settled there in the late 18th century.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: For couples coming from Boston, the drive north on I-95 through New Hampshire and into Maine takes about three hours before arriving in Fayette. The route through Augusta and into the central Maine lakes region is a genuine transition — by the time guests turn onto South Road, they've passed several lakes and the landscape has shifted completely from highway to countryside. Augusta has hotels from most major chains and a legitimate downtown dining scene on Water Street. For guests coming from Burlington or Montreal, Fayette sits at a convenient midpoint in New England — accessible from multiple directions without requiring a flight.

title: Maine Wedding Venues with On-Site Accommodations
headline: The Venue and the Farmhouse. Both Yours.
subhead: The farmhouse at South Road Farm sleeps 10. It's included in every weekend rental.
description: Looking for Maine wedding venues with on-site accommodations? South Road Farm includes a fully restored farmhouse sleeping up to 10 guests with every weekend rental — no extra charge, no separate booking.
heroImageAlt: The farmhouse at South Road Farm
intro: Every weekend rental at South Road Farm includes the farmhouse — four bedrooms, sleeping up to 10 guests, yours from Friday through Sunday. It's not an add-on or an upgrade. It's part of what the weekend is. Waking up Saturday morning on the property, making breakfast before the ceremony, coming back to the farmhouse after the dancing stops — that's what separates a weekend from a single day.
proximity: Located in Fayette, Maine. Additional accommodations available in Augusta (30 min) and the central Maine lakes region.

bodyContent.heading: What the farmhouse actually is
bodyContent.text: The farmhouse is a fully restored four-bedroom home on the property — not a bunkhouse or a converted outbuilding. It has a fully equipped kitchen, multiple bathrooms, living spaces, and a deck overlooking the land. It sleeps up to 10 guests comfortably. Most couples house the wedding party or immediate family there, though the configuration is entirely up to you.

bodyContent.heading: Why on-site accommodations change everything
bodyContent.text: When key people stay on the property, the wedding weekend stops being a series of logistics and starts being an experience. The wedding party gets ready together Saturday morning on the farm. Late night ends not with a shuttle to a hotel but with people gathering on the farmhouse porch. Sunday breakfast happens in the place where everything else happened. For couples who want their wedding to feel genuinely immersive, having accommodation on-site is the single biggest factor.

quote.text: South Road Farm is where I found my way back to my roots. Years after leaving Wayne, I returned to my aunt and uncle's farm in Fayette for my wedding — a four-day celebration grounded in family, friends, and tradition.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: The farmhouse at South Road Farm sits within one of Maine's most lake-dense regions — Echo Lake, Parker Pond, and Flying Pond are all within a few miles, and loons calling across the water at dusk is something overnight guests consistently mention. For guests not staying on-site, Augusta (30 minutes) has hotel blocks from Hampton Inn, Homewood Suites, and several other chains. The Fayette and Wayne area has numerous Airbnbs and lake cottages available in summer and fall — many couples put together a lodging guide pointing guests toward options around Echo Lake for a more immersive experience.

title: Rustic Wedding Venues in Maine
headline: Rustic Without the Quotation Marks
subhead: Post-and-beam construction from the early 1800s. Still standing. Restored by hand.
description: Searching for rustic wedding venues in Maine? South Road Farm features two authentically restored 19th-century barns on 66 acres in Fayette, Maine — genuine historic construction, not a renovated event space built to look the part.
heroImageAlt: Renovation work at South Road Farm showing the barn structure
intro: A lot of venues use 'rustic' to mean reclaimed wood paneling and Edison bulbs installed in 2019. The barns at South Road Farm are rustic because they're original — post-and-beam framing from the early 1800s, granite foundation hauled by oxen from quarries in Jay, Maine. They were restored by Dave, a carpenter and co-owner, who treated them the way you treat something you'll stand in at a wedding yourself.
proximity: Located in Fayette, Maine — central Maine's lakes region.

bodyContent.heading: What authentic construction actually looks like
bodyContent.text: The main barn at South Road Farm has post-and-beam framing that dates to the early 1800s. The granite foundation was hauled by oxen from quarries in Jay. The structure survived nearly two centuries before it came into the current owners' hands — and then survived a once-in-a-generation windstorm that took the entire roof off during the restoration. Not just the shingles. The full timber framing. The team rebuilt it rather than replace it, because this is their property and they wanted it done right.

bodyContent.heading: The difference between rustic and rough
bodyContent.text: Authentic doesn't mean uncomfortable. The barns are fully wired for lighting and sound. The farmhouse has a proper kitchen, multiple bathrooms, and all the amenities you'd want for a weekend stay. The grounds are maintained and well-considered. The rustic quality lives in the materials — the old timber, the irregular geometry, the texture of something made by hand — not in a lack of care or comfort.

quote.text: The moonrise over the barn and stable transported me to a time when its pale light stretched a farmer's day.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: Fayette has a population of about 1,100 people. It has a country store, a chain of lakes, and roads that wind through farmland and second-growth forest that feel genuinely remote without being inaccessible. This is the landscape the barns at South Road Farm were built for — not a curated rural aesthetic, but the actual central Maine countryside. The Kennebec Highlands trail network is nearby. Echo Lake's public beach is a short drive. Augusta is 30 minutes away when guests need a proper meal or a hotel. The combination of genuine remoteness and easy access is what makes this part of Maine work for destination weddings — far enough to feel somewhere, close enough that no one has to wonder how they'll get back.

title: Private Estate Wedding Venues in Maine
headline: Exclusively Yours. All Weekend.
subhead: 66 acres, two barns, a farmhouse, and no other weddings. Ever on the same weekend.
description: Looking for private estate wedding venues in Maine? South Road Farm is a 66-acre private property in Fayette, Maine offering exclusive full-weekend rentals — one couple, one weekend, the entire property.
heroImageAlt: The restored reception barn at South Road Farm
intro: Private means different things at different venues. At South Road Farm it means: no other couples on the property, no shared parking lot, no timeline built around someone else's event. The 66 acres are yours from Friday afternoon to Sunday morning. The ceremony lawn, both barns, the courtyard, the farmhouse. One wedding per weekend, by design.
proximity: Located at 220 South Road, Fayette, Maine. Available May through October.

bodyContent.heading: What one wedding per weekend actually means
bodyContent.text: Many venues describe themselves as private and then sell multiple events on the same weekend — different sections of the property, staggered timing, shared parking. South Road Farm hosts one wedding per weekend, full stop. There is no other event happening while you're there. The 66 acres are entirely yours — no need to stay in 'your' area, no strangers visible in the background of photos, no staff managing logistics for a parallel event.

bodyContent.heading: The estate experience at a non-estate price
bodyContent.text: Private estate venues — the kind with long driveways, historic houses, and grounds you've seen in magazines — typically start at $20,000 and go well beyond that. South Road Farm offers the same essential experience: a private property exclusively yours for the weekend, with on-site accommodations, at a price point that reflects being in central Maine rather than the Hamptons or Hudson Valley. Starting at $12,000 for the full weekend including the farmhouse.

quote.text: After the wedding, I kept returning to the farm, drawn back again and again. Through South Road Farm, the whispers of my ancestors brought me home.
quote.attribution: Dyanna Lincoln, whose family farmed this land for decades

localContext: South Road Farm's 66 acres occupy a quiet road in Fayette, surrounded by the lakes and forests of central Maine. Parker Pond and Echo Lake are within a few miles. The nearest neighbor is far enough away that the property feels completely private — which it is. Augusta, 30 minutes south, provides the nearest hotels (Hampton Inn, Homewood Suites, and others along Western Avenue) and the best restaurant options for ancillary events. Otto's on the River and The Oak Table & Bar on Water Street are both well-suited for rehearsal dinners. Cushnoc Brewing Company works well for a casual welcome gathering. The combination of an entirely private property and a genuine small city 30 minutes away gives couples the privacy they want without leaving guests stranded.
